spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates polygon(round <length>) syntax across 5 shape archetypes (triangle, diamond, hexagon, arrow, star), and animates clip-path between sharp and rounded via both :hover transition and @keyframes — the headline use case for making rounding animatable.

interactive.rationale: Shape selector + radius range + duration range + play/pause control all drive a live stage with three side-by-side variants and a live code readout.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Lede promises animated clip-path with the rounding parameter; delivered with multiple interaction modes (hover, keyframe loop).

edge_cases.rationale: Good shape variety, but no demo of the spec's per-vertex clamping rule (radius capped to tan(angle/2)*segment/2), no concave-corner edge case, no zero-radius animation (which should be identical to no-round), and no demonstration that fill-rule interacts with round.

design_system.rationale: All chrome uses CSS variables, the play/pause button overrides background AND color together, no static-card pattern, CSS.supports gates the support banner.

openQuestions.title: Show per-vertex clamping
openQuestions.detail: The spec clamps the radius per vertex to tan(angle/2)*segment/2. On the star (acute vertices) this is visibly different from the slider value. Add an 'effective radius' readout per shape showing what the renderer is actually applying. Authors otherwise get confused when their 50px slider produces only 6px of rounding on a tight star point.

openQuestions.title: Animate vertex count, not just radius
openQuestions.detail: polygon(round Npx) animation between shapes with different vertex counts is undefined / discontinuous in the spec. Add an explicit demo of triangle→diamond (3→4 vertices) failing to interpolate, paired with the round-radius animation that does work, to make the spec boundary explicit.

openQuestions.title: Demo offset-path / shape-outside angle
openQuestions.detail: polygon(round) is allowed in offset-path and shape-outside too. Today the demo only exercises clip-path. Adding even a 60-line offset-path variant (e.g. a marker travelling around a rounded star) makes the page show the surface area more honestly.

openQuestions.title: Show negative-test (round 0)
openQuestions.detail: polygon(round 0px, ...) should render identical to polygon(...). Add a third side-by-side that proves this for sanity, and to expose any rendering bugs.

summary: Strong animation demo across five shapes with hover + keyframe variants and a live CSS readout. Two real gaps: the spec's per-vertex radius clamping is invisible, and the animation-between-different-shapes failure case isn't shown — both would help authors avoid surprises.
